steps.title: Keep the phrase unbroken
steps.content: The em dashes already set off the parenthetical phrase. Inside it, including is a preposition, so its object should follow directly without punctuation interrupting the phrase.

steps.title: Reject added punctuation
steps.content: Choice B adds an extra em dash inside an already dashed parenthetical. Choice C uses a colon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. Choice D puts a comma between the preposition and its object.

steps.title: Leave the object direct
steps.content: Choice A leaves the phrase as including the languages Madurese and Bikol, with no punctuation separating including from its object. The answer is A.
